name: [BUG-ANA-004] TC-ANA-004 — mcda_id=invalid_value returns 200 instead of 400 (no input validation)
description:
**[BUG-ANA-004]**

The `mcda_id` query parameter is supposed to filter analytics data by MCDA organisation.

**Test scenarios:**
- `?mcda_id=PLATFORM` → 200 with `mcda_id: "ALL"` (filter ignored, no error)
- `?mcda_id=invalid_xyz` → 200 with `mcda_id: "ALL"` (invalid value silently ignored)
- `?mcda_id=` (empty) → 200 with `mcda_id: "ALL"` (empty silently ignored)

**Expected:**
- Valid known mcda_id → returns filtered data with `mcda_id` echoed back
- Unknown mcda_id → 400 Bad Request (not silently fall back to ALL)

**Impact:** Filtering is completely non-functional. API consumers cannot scope analytics to a specific MCDA organisation. Invalid values never trigger validation errors.
descriptionHtml:
[BUG-ANA-004]

The mcda_id query parameter is supposed to filter analytics data by MCDA organisation.

Test scenarios:

  • ?mcda_id=PLATFORM → 200 with mcda_id: "ALL" (filter ignored, no error)
  • ?mcda_id=invalid_xyz → 200 with mcda_id: "ALL" (invalid value silently ignored)
  • ?mcda_id= (empty) → 200 with mcda_id: "ALL" (empty silently ignored)

Expected:

  • Valid known mcda_id → returns filtered data with mcda_id echoed back
  • Unknown mcda_id → 400 Bad Request (not silently fall back to ALL)

Impact: Filtering is completely non-functional. API consumers cannot scope analytics to a specific MCDA organisation. Invalid values never trigger validation errors.

expect(received).toBe(expected) // Object.is equality

Expected: "PLATFORM"
